heading: Milsons Passage vs Milsons Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Milsons Passage, New South Wales and Milsons Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Milsons Passage has a lower overall climate risk score (45/100) compared to Milsons Point (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Milsons Passage or Milsons Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Milsons Passage has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Milsons Point scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Milsons Passage scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Milsons Point scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Milsons Passage or Milsons Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Milsons Passage has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.27% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
